title: Kohleria inaequalis
intro: Kohleria inaequalis, a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Kohleria inaequalis include iridoids, phenolic compounds, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Kohleria inaequalis is commonly prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used to treat colds, sore throats, and coughs.

The Gesneriaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that also includes the genera Gloxinia and Sinningia.
